name_en: Mojahedin of the Islamic Revolution Organization
description: The Mojahedin of the Islamic Revolution Organization (OMIIR) is a reformist party reconstituted in 1991 from the left wing of the original 1979 organisation of the same name. It is closely allied with the [Islamic Iran Participation Front](/orgs/islamic-iran-participation-front) within the reform movement. Key figures include [Behzad Nabavi](/people/behzad-nabavi), [Mohsen Armin](/people/mohsen-armin), and [Mostafa Tajzadeh](/people/mostafa-tajzadeh). The party emphasised the republican dimensions of the Islamic Republic over its theocratic elements. Several members were arrested and imprisoned after the 2009 [Green Movement](/events/green-movement-protests-2009) protests.
editorial_notes: Reconstituted 1991 from the left wing of the original 1979 organisation. Key figures: Nabavi, Armin, Tajzadeh. Several senior members imprisoned post-2009.
